result:
name things for what they do, not how they do it. if name needs "And" or "Helper" or "Manager", split it.

keep functions small enough you see whole thing without scrolling. if you can't hold it in head, neither can future-you.

group by feature, not by layer. all auth stuff together beats "all controllers here, all models there".

push side effects to edges. pure logic in middle, i/o at boundaries. spaghetti usually means db calls tangled inside business rules.

delete dead code immediately. commented-out blocks, unused helpers, "might need later" — gone. uncertainty rots readability.

when lost, draw data flow on paper before touching code. if arrows cross or loop back, that's your spaghetti. straighten there.

one rule: if explaining function to yourself takes more than one sentence, function does too much.
